question: How many distinct users have overdue assigned training?
sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t.transc_user_id) AS overdue_user_count
FROM transcript_core AS t
WHERE t.is_assigned = TRUE
  AND t.is_removed = FALSE
  AND t.is_latest_reg_num = TRUE
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date < CURRENT_DATE
  AND (t.user_lo_comp_dt IS NULL OR t.user_lo_comp_dt > t.user_lo_min_due_date);
instructions: The per-user assignment record lives on transcript_core, NOT on training_assignment_core (which has no user column). A user's training is overdue when the transcript's per-user due date (user_lo_min_due_date) is in the past and it is either incomplete (user_lo_comp_dt IS NULL) or was completed late (user_lo_comp_dt > user_lo_min_due_date). The user key is transc_user_id; reg_num is a registration-attempt counter, NOT a user id — never COUNT(DISTINCT reg_num) for users. Deduplicate re-registrations with is_latest_reg_num = TRUE and exclude removed items with is_removed = FALSE. 'Assigned' = is_assigned = TRUE.

question: How many users are overdue on their assigned training, compared to users who completed training on time?
sql:
WITH latest_assigned AS (
  SELECT t.transc_user_id, t.user_lo_min_due_date, t.user_lo_comp_dt
  FROM transcript_core AS t
  WHERE t.is_assigned = TRUE
    AND t.is_removed = FALSE
    AND t.is_latest_reg_num = TRUE
    AND t.user_lo_min_due_date IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT transc_user_id) FILTER (
    WHERE user_lo_min_due_date < CURRENT_DATE
      AND (user_lo_comp_dt IS NULL OR user_lo_comp_dt > user_lo_min_due_date)
  ) AS overdue_users,
  COUNT(DISTINCT transc_user_id) FILTER (
    WHERE user_lo_comp_dt IS NOT NULL
      AND user_lo_comp_dt <= user_lo_min_due_date
  ) AS completed_on_time_users
FROM latest_assigned;
instructions: A comparison question returns exactly two values: one count of overdue users and one count of on-time users. Compute both from transcript_core in a single pass using COUNT(DISTINCT transc_user_id) FILTER (...). Overdue = due date passed and not completed (or completed after the due date). On time = completed on or before the per-user due date (user_lo_min_due_date). Do NOT join transcript_core to training_assignment_core on object_id to count users — that join is on the course (object_id), produces a many-to-many cartesian product across all users of the course, and double-counts. transcript_core alone already encodes the per-user assignment.

question: List the users who are overdue on mandatory training, with their names and email.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.user_id, u.user_name_first, u.user_name_last, u.user_email
FROM transcript_core AS t
JOIN users_core AS u ON u.user_id = t.transc_user_id
WHERE t.is_assigned = TRUE
  AND t.is_required = TRUE
  AND t.is_removed = FALSE
  AND t.is_latest_reg_num = TRUE
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date < CURRENT_DATE
  AND (t.user_lo_comp_dt IS NULL OR t.user_lo_comp_dt > t.user_lo_min_due_date);
instructions: Join transcript_core to users_core on users_core.user_id = transcript_core.transc_user_id (transc_user_id is the user foreign key; user_id is the users_core primary key). 'Mandatory'/'required' training is is_required = TRUE; 'assigned' is is_assigned = TRUE. Use the per-user due date user_lo_min_due_date for the overdue test. SELECT DISTINCT on the user columns so a user with several overdue items appears once.

question: How many users have overdue assigned training, broken down by organizational unit?
sql:
SELECT o.ou_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.transc_user_id) AS overdue_user_count
FROM transcript_core AS t
JOIN user_ou_core AS uo ON uo.user_id = t.transc_user_id
JOIN ou_core AS o ON o.ou_id = uo.ou_id
WHERE t.is_assigned = TRUE
  AND t.is_removed = FALSE
  AND t.is_latest_reg_num = TRUE
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND t.user_lo_min_due_date < CURRENT_DATE
  AND (t.user_lo_comp_dt IS NULL OR t.user_lo_comp_dt > t.user_lo_min_due_date)
GROUP BY o.ou_id
ORDER BY overdue_user_count DESC;
instructions: Organizational unit membership is the bridge user_ou_core (user_id, ou_id) joined to ou_core (ou_id is the primary key). Chain: transcript_core.transc_user_id = user_ou_core.user_id, and user_ou_core.ou_id = ou_core.ou_id. Count distinct users per OU. A user may belong to multiple OUs, which can attribute them to more than one unit — that is expected for an OU rollup.

question: What is the on-time completion rate for assigned training?
sql:
WITH latest_assigned AS (
  SELECT t.transc_user_id, t.user_lo_min_due_date, t.user_lo_comp_dt
  FROM transcript_core AS t
  WHERE t.is_assigned = TRUE
    AND t.is_removed = FALSE
    AND t.is_latest_reg_num = TRUE
    AND t.user_lo_min_due_date IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (
    WHERE user_lo_comp_dt IS NOT NULL AND user_lo_comp_dt <= user_lo_min_due_date
  )::numeric
  / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS on_time_completion_rate
FROM latest_assigned;
instructions: Completion-rate questions are a ratio: on-time completions over total assigned items. Use the per-assignment grain (one row per latest, non-removed assigned transcript item) rather than per-user here, because a rate is over assignments. On time = user_lo_comp_dt IS NOT NULL AND user_lo_comp_dt <= user_lo_min_due_date. Cast to numeric and guard the denominator with NULLIF(..., 0) to avoid divide-by-zero.
